Tests that `Math.tan` returns `NaN` when given `undefined` as input.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that checks the behavior of the `Math.tan` function when it receives `undefined` as an argument. The script uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of `Math.tan(undefined)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). This test ensures that the `Math.tan` function correctly handles invalid input by returning `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `Math.tan` function.
